title: Desktop App Config Resolution Surface Area

packages_app_src_config.note: Directory packages/app/src/config/ does NOT exist. The renderer web app has zero Electron imports and no config module of its own.

desktop_main_config_files.exports: CHANNEL, APP_IDS, UPDATER_ENABLED, store key constants
getStore() — lazy electron-store singleton per namespace
getDefaultServerUrl, setDefaultServerUrl, getWslConfig, setWslConfig, preferAppEnv, spawnLocalServer

desktop_main_config_files.xdg_overrides: XDG_DATA/CONFIG/CACHE/STATE_HOME + OPENCODE_DB=:memory: for onboarding tests lines 126-130

opencode_src_config.barrel_file: No barrel — each file exports its own namespace via `export * as ConfigX from './x'`
opencode_src_config.loading_priority: 10 levels from well-known remote → global config file → project .opencode/ → env vars → managed/MDM
opencode_src_config.validation: Effect Schema declarative validation with parse/error reporting

system_a.name: Desktop Shell Config (electron-store)
system_a.scope: Electron app preferences: window state, WSL, display backend, plugins, custom agents, MCP servers, locale, zoom

system_b.name: Backend Opencode Config (Effect Schema)
system_b.scope: Runtime configuration: providers, models, agents, commands, MCP definitions, permissions, shell, logLevel, server, share, etc.
system_b.storage: JSONC files in XDG config dirs (~/.config/opencode/) and project .opencode/ directories
system_b.schema: Effect Schema — full declarative validation (Config.Info type)
system_b.access_by_renderer: HTTP API via SDK — NOT IPC

config_flow_diagram.backend_config: Sidecar Server (Effect Schema) ──HTTP──▶ SDK Client ──TanStack Query──▶ SolidJS Store ──context──▶ Components
config_flow_diagram.note: These are TWO SEPARATE PATHS. IPC config is for Electron shell preferences. HTTP config is for opencode runtime settings. They don't share a data model.

preload_bridge_summary.config_methods_available_to_renderer: storeGet/Set/Delete/Clear/Keys/Length, getWindowConfig, getDefaultServerUrl, getWslConfig, getDisplayBackend, getDesktopPluginConfig, getCustomAgents, getMcpServers, getLocalePreference

new_untracked.purpose: Centralizes app identity, GitHub OAuth, protocol constants
Centralized IPC channel registry — single source of truth

smoking_guns.detail: The directory the user asked about doesn't exist. Config is split across TWO completely separate systems: (A) Desktop shell config via electron-store/IPC and (B) Backend opencode config via Effect Schema/HTTP.

smoking_guns.finding: Two completely separate config systems with different access patterns, storage, and validation
smoking_guns.detail: Desktop config: electron-store (no schema, IPC bridge). Backend config: Effect Schema (validated, HTTP API). They don't share data models. A developer looking for 'the config' needs to understand which system they need.

smoking_guns.finding: Desktop electron-store has NO schema validation or type safety (beyond store key strings)
smoking_guns.detail: window.api.storeSet(namespace, key, value) accepts any key/value. The renderer wraps it in a generic AsyncStorage. No type checks, no migration strategy for shape changes.

smoking_guns.finding: Stale preload types after SQLite overlay removal (SqliteMigrationProgress, InitStep 'sqlite_waiting')
Backend config consumers in the desktop app read config via SDK HTTP API, NOT via IPC
smoking_guns.detail: Config readers like settings-general.tsx use 'serverSync.data.config.shell' (TanStack Query → SDK HTTP API). They never call window.api.* for backend config.

smoking_guns.finding: Main process sets userData path override — critical for electron-store placement
smoking_guns.detail: index.ts:135-137 sets app.setPath('userData', join(app.getPath('appData'), appId)). This MUST happen before any getStore() call due to module hoisting timing (documented in store.ts comment).
